lines.text: Welcome back. The clock reads 23:00. We are {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
I'm Sister Marigold. This is the wellness block. [pause] Find a place where you can sit without moving for the next few minutes.
Close your eyes if you feel safe doing that. If you don't feel safe, keep them open. Both are acceptable.
Let's begin with the breath. Breathe in [pause] through your nose. [pause] Hold for four counts.
One. [pause] Two. [pause] Three. [pause] Four.
Now release through your mouth. Let it go completely.
Again. In through the nose. [pause] Hold. [pause] Release.
Your body is in a safe space. You chose this space. You locked the door. You barricaded the window.
The weight you're feeling in your chest — that's not danger. That's awareness. That's good.
Breathe in. [pause] Your shoulders are tight. Notice that. Don't change it. Just notice.
The phone has rung more tonight than it has in years. Tucker mentioned that earlier. Did you hear?
More callers than usual. More voices asking to be heard. More people alone in rooms like yours.
Breathe out. [pause] You are not alone in your solitude. That's a paradox, but it's true.
Release your jaw. You've been clenching it. [pause] Let it hang loose.
Imagine the person you're most afraid of right now. [pause] I'm not asking you to name them.
Just picture them. Somewhere outside. [pause] And now picture the door between you and them.
That door is very solid. That door is yours.
Breathe in. The night has been loud from 7 to now. Field reports. Cut-offs. Tucker's voice breaking up and coming back.
Noise. [pause] Constant noise. Your ears have been listening.
Now let them rest. Breathe in. [pause] There is sound inside this room with you. Count them.
The hum of the heater. The settling of the house. Your heartbeat if you listen for it.
These are safe sounds. [pause] These are your sounds.
Release. [pause] Notice your hands. Are they cold? Warm? Are they shaking?
Whatever you're feeling is correct. [pause] Your body knows what it needs to know.
There's a caller right now — somewhere in Madison — listening to my voice instead of hearing the sirens.
I want them to know: that choice was brave. [pause] That choice was the right one.
Breathe in. [pause] Think about 7 o'clock this morning. When the sun came up.
It came up. [pause] It always does. The night before it came up, people did what people do.
And the sun came up anyway. [pause] It will come up tomorrow.
Not because of anything you did. [pause] Not because of anything anyone did.
It will come up because that's what suns do. [pause] That's their job.
Your job tonight is smaller. [pause] Your job is to breathe.
In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
There are people who will not see 7 o'clock tomorrow. [pause] I don't know them. You don't know them.
But they are real. [pause] And the fact that you are breathing right now — breathing safely — matters.
It matters that you chose the door. [pause] It matters that you locked it.
shé-ka-lummé-shé [pause] forgive the breath. Continue breathing.
Release any thought about what you should be feeling. [pause] Feel what you feel.
Guilt. [pause] Relief. [pause] Anger. [pause] Gratitude. [pause] All of it is welcome here.
Your Order — I mean, your own order, your own rules for tonight — is sacred.
You set them. You keep them. No one else knows what you promised yourself when the clock hit 7.
Breathe in. [pause] That promise is still good.
We are {{ELAPSED}} in. [pause] Eight hours remain.
Eight hours is a long time and no time at all. [pause] Eight hours contains multitudes.
But you don't have to think about eight hours. [pause] Think about the next breath.
In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
I'm going to be here. [pause] Quinn will return. [pause] Tucker will report. [pause] Nick will play songs.
You are held in a structure. [pause] Structures are safe.
The structure of this broadcast. [pause] The structure of this night. [pause] The structure of time itself.
Breathe in. [pause] You are safe in your locked room. [pause] And you are not alone.
That's enough for now. [pause] Stay with us.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide cumulative: 61 kills (22:00 hour). East Side continuing to spike. +23% vs this point last year.
Phone lines busier than normal. Tim reports 340+ active calls in queue. Listeners staying tuned for company.
"You are held in a structure. Structures are safe."
Capitol Square perimeter holding as of 22:50. Fortification at inner ring intact. Caroline: cold front arriving 00:30-01:30.
